response: Tymnet was an international data communications network that utilized virtual call packet switched technology. It connected host computers (servers) at numerous large companies, educational institutions, and government agencies using various interfaces such as X.25, SNA/SDLC, BSC, and ASCII. Users generally connected to Tymnet through dial-up or dedicated asynchronous connections. The business model of Tymnet included both a large public network for dial-up users and a private network service for government agencies and large companies, particularly banks and airlines, to create their own dedicated networks. These private networks could be interconnected with the public network via gateways. Additionally, Tymnet was interconnected with many other public networks both in the U.S. and internationally through X.25/X.75 gateways.
expected: an international data communications network headquartered in San Jose, CA
